ksa_text: Knowledge of model monitoring requirements, including data drift detection, prediction drift, performance degradation metrics, input data distribution monitoring, latency and throughput tracking, alerting thresholds, and retraining triggers appropriate for different model types and serving patterns.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: AI/ML Engineering is a primary or secondary domain for the Director of AI/ML. This knowledge KSA is within scope for the role's expected competency profile and seniority level (director).

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope (AI/ML Engineering), seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with inclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Framework signal is weak (composite 0.407). External validation of this knowledge KSA for the Director of AI/ML role relies primarily on domain-level alignment rather than specific framework matches.
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: O*NET, DCWF, and ML engineering job market data confirm AI/ML competencies for technical data leadership roles.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: domain-level relevance of AI/ML Engineering for the Director of AI/ML is corroborated by DAMA-DMBOK, CDO survey data, and role-specific job market analysis, despite weak cross-framework signal.

decision_rationale: AI/ML Engineering knowledge KSA is within scope for the Director of AI/ML at the director tier. Seniority fit is appropriate. All three evaluation passes support inclusion.
